title: Understanding the Force
text: The force acting on the particle is given as $$18$$ N in the i direction. This means the force is constant and acts in a straight line.

title: Using Newton's Second Law
text: According to Newton's second law, the acceleration can be calculated using the formula: $$F=ma$$. Here, F is the force, $$m$$ is the mass, and a is the acceleration.

title: Calculate the Acceleration
text: Use the formula $$F=ma$$ to find the acceleration of the particle. Given $$F=18$$ N and $$m=2$$ kg, calculate a.

title: Kinematic Equation for Distance
text: To find the distance traveled, use the kinematic equation: $$d=ut+0.5{at}^2$$, where u is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Calculate the Distance
text: Given that the initial velocity $$u=0$$, acceleration $$a=9$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$, and time $$t=5$$ s, calculate the distance using the formula $$d=ut+0.5{at}^2$$.
